Identity.Name: StingColorSchemes
Identity.Definition: Named colour schemes used by ColorByParameter, drawing-type Print overrides, and view-style-pack PrintOverride.ColorScheme. Each scheme maps an element's parameter value to a colour using a documented palette. Drives both element graphic overrides and tag-text colour switching.

Governance.StandardsBasis: STING ColorHelper palettes (Select/ColorCommands.cs); STING TagStyleEngine ColorScheme catalogue; STING DrawingType PrintOverride.ColorScheme

Value.Definition: Colour by discipline — M=blue, E=yellow, P=green, A=grey, S=red, FP=orange, LV=purple, G=brown, H=teal, MG=cyan, RP=magenta.
Red / Amber / Green status — used for compliance, completeness, condition-rating views.
Spectral / rainbow gradient — used for continuous-value parameters (load, temperature, dB).
Warm palette — reds, oranges, yellows. Heat / fire / cooking-load mapping.
Cool palette — blues, cyans, teals. Cooling / flow / water mapping.
Monochrome — black / grey / white only. Print-friendly, photocopier-safe.
High contrast — saturated primaries with strong outlines. QA / checking views.
Colourblind-safe palette (viridis-like). Universal accessibility for client presentations.
Rich presentation palette — full saturation, material-true colours. Client-facing rendered drawings.
Monochrome presentation — burgundy / sage / cream variants. Client-facing minimalist drawings.
Clarification / RFI palette — red markup callouts on greyscale base. Used by all clarification-pack drawing types.
